title: silene vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as catchfly or white campion, is a perennial plant in the carnation family, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Silene vulgaris subsp. vulgaris include its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic to reduce urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
